description: Orchestrator skill registry — contains skills with full I/O contracts, feedback routes, and orchestration metadata required for pipeline routing. This is a curated subset of skills/index.yaml; not every skill needs a registry entry (only those the orchestrator needs to resolve explicitly). The complete skill catalog is in skills/index.yaml.

parallel_groups.description: Code review, testing strategy, and security review run concurrently during implementation phase
parallel_groups.note: Priority: clean-code-review output is consumed by performance-guard (downstream), so it must complete before the guard layer. Parallel execution is safe because these three skills share no direct output dependency.

parallel_groups.description: Telemetry collection and insight aggregation run asynchronously at pipeline.ended — non-blocking
parallel_groups.note: Priority: session-insights depends on behavioral-telemetry-collector output (session_summary). These run sequentially within the async group, not truly in parallel.

skills.name: requirement-analyzer
skills.description: Extracts, normalizes, and validates requirements from raw input
skills.orchestration: MUST run first — entry point for all feature work

skills.name: adaptive-proposal-generator
skills.description: Analyzes session_summary from session-insights (SKL-048) to generate ranked AdaptationProposal objects covering new skills, skill modifications, new pipeline templates, and agent changes. All proposals require HITL approval — nothing is applied automatically.
skills.orchestration: Invoked at session end after session-insights completes; async, non-blocking. Presents proposals to HITL gate before routing to adaptation-applicator.

skills.name: multi-agent-debate
skills.description: Runs an adversarial Architect vs. Reviewer debate loop to produce a critique-hardened architecture artifact before feature planning begins. Inspired by AutoGen ConversableAgent pattern. Optional phase enabled by pipeline_config.debate_architecture: true. Quality-scored 87/100; HITL-approved for registration.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after phase-2-architecture and before phase-2b-design. Optional — enabled by pipeline_config.debate_architecture: true. Escalates to HITL gate on stalemate or max_rounds exceeded.

feedback_routes.description: Passes unresolved_concerns[] as architecture_risks[] to feature-planning

skills.name: realtime-system-architect
skills.description: Use when designing real-time systems with WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or WebRTC. Triggers on: "real-time architecture", "WebSocket design", "WebRTC", "collaborative editing", "CRDT", "presence system". Do NOT use for batch or polling-based systems. Do NOT use for simple REST APIs.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when use cases include real-time collaboration, live feeds, or bidirectional streaming

feedback_routes.description: HITL when connection scale requires multi-region WebSocket routing with sticky sessions
